goal: Push DevDrop from local-first MVP into an ambitious capstone product. Add release packaging, executable demo verification, diagnostics, manifest diff/reconcile preview, hosted manifest sync, daemon/watch mode, FUSE lazy workspace mounting, team secret sharing, explicit dependency install, and final proof artifacts. Push the bounds of AI-assisted delivery while keeping every risky capability explicit, inspectable, reversible, and free of real secrets in docs, fixtures, logs, or tests.

cards.title: DevDrop: release packaging and install docs
cards.task: Add release packaging for the `devspace` CLI. Provide a Makefile or scripts that run tests, vet, build a local binary, build release artifacts for the current platform, and generate SHA256 checksums. Document how to install from source and how a GitHub Release should be assembled. Keep the implementation simple and repo-native; do not introduce a hosted service or heavyweight release framework unless it materially reduces complexity.
cards.scope: Touch release scripts, Makefile, README/install docs, and tests only as needed for packaging verification. Do not rename the Go module or change product behavior except where command naming is necessary for release packaging.

cards.title: DevDrop: executable demo-check harness
cards.task: Turn the capstone two-machine demo into an executable script, for example `scripts/demo-check.sh`. It must build `devspace`, create temporary workspaces and local bare Git remotes, run init/scan/push/pull/plan/apply/hydrate/env/status, assert the generated `.env` is mode 0600, and cleanly report success. Update docs so the final demo script and proof artifacts point at this command.
cards.scope: Create a deterministic local harness using temporary directories only. Do not require network access, GitHub auth, Linear auth, or real secrets. Keep generated temporary files outside the repo unless the user passes an explicit output path.
dependsOn: DevDrop: release packaging and install docs

cards.title: DevDrop: doctor diagnostics command
cards.task: Add `devspace doctor` to diagnose local readiness. It should check config presence, workspace manifest validity, workspace path safety, age identity readability, Git availability, manifest remote configuration, manifest repo cleanliness if configured, last plan presence/hash status if available, and whether tracked project paths are missing, placeholders, hydrated, dirty, or missing env files. Output human-readable results and return non-zero only for hard failures that prevent core commands from working.
cards.scope: Add a read-only diagnostics command and focused tests. Do not mutate config, clone repos, pull remotes, write env files, install dependencies, or contact hosted services except for existing local Git checks needed to inspect configured state.

cards.title: DevDrop: manifest remote diff preview
cards.task: Add a safe preview command for comparing the local workspace manifest with the configured remote manifest, for example `devspace workspace diff`. It should fetch/read the configured manifest repo cache, validate the remote manifest, localize paths for comparison, and report added, removed, and changed projects without replacing the local manifest. This gives users a reconciliation preview before `workspace pull`.
cards.scope: Read-only diff preview only. Do not implement force pull, merge UI, automatic conflict resolution, remote writes, or source-code repo operations in this card. Include tests with local bare Git remotes and invalid remote manifest cases.

cards.title: DevDrop: hosted manifest sync control plane
cards.task: Push DevDrop beyond Git-only manifest sync by adding a hosted sync control-plane prototype for workspace manifests. Define the API surface, auth model, storage model, sync versioning, conflict behavior, and local fallback behavior. Implement a minimal hosted sync path if the repo shape supports it, or create a runnable local server prototype if a full hosted deployment is too large for one card. Add CLI configuration for hosted sync endpoint and auth token. Add hosted push/pull behavior or explicit hosted subcommands if that keeps behavior clearer. Preserve Git-backed sync as a fallback and document what is stored remotely.
cards.scope: Hosted sync is opt-in. Never upload source code, dependency folders, `.env` files, or plaintext secrets. Reject unsafe project paths server-side and client-side. Include conflict detection and do not silently overwrite local manifest changes. If implementation is too large, ship a runnable spike with exact follow-up cards.

cards.title: DevDrop: background daemon and workspace watcher
cards.task: Add a background daemon or long-running `devspace watch` mode so DevDrop can keep workspace metadata fresh without users remembering manual scans. Watch configured workspace paths for project additions/removals and manifest-relevant changes, debounce filesystem events, refresh manifest/state safely, and define how watch mode interacts with hosted sync and Git-backed sync.
cards.scope: Do not auto-delete projects, auto-install dependencies, auto-hydrate repositories without opt-in, upload secrets, or spin CPU on large dependency folders. Preserve existing ignore rules and keep watch behavior demonstrable locally.
dependsOn: DevDrop: hosted manifest sync control plane

cards.title: DevDrop: FUSE lazy workspace mount prototype
cards.task: Prototype a FUSE-backed workspace mount that exposes tracked projects before they are fully hydrated and can trigger safe hydration on demand. Evaluate the best Go-compatible FUSE library, add a prototype command such as `devspace mount <mountpoint>`, represent tracked projects from the manifest as mount entries, and document platform requirements and fallback behavior.
cards.scope: Treat this as a prototype card. Do not delete local files, clone into non-empty destinations, hide hydration failures, or require FUSE for normal CLI workflows. If full FUSE is too risky, land a documented runnable spike and create follow-up implementation cards.
dependsOn: DevDrop: background daemon and workspace watcher

cards.title: DevDrop: team secret sharing and access model
cards.task: Add team-oriented encrypted secret profile sharing so DevDrop can support realistic multi-developer workspace recovery without exposing plaintext secrets. Define users, teams, project access, recipients, and revocation behavior. Extend env profile storage to support sharing encrypted values with multiple recipients. Add CLI flows for exporting/inviting/rotating recipients if that fits the architecture.
cards.scope: Never send plaintext secrets to hosted services. Never print secret values in logs, status, errors, docs, or tests. Define revocation limitations honestly and preserve local-only env profile behavior for solo users.
dependsOn: DevDrop: hosted manifest sync control plane

cards.title: DevDrop: explicit dependency install workflow
cards.task: Add a dependency install workflow that turns DevDrop setup hints into explicit, reviewable actions. Add a command such as `devspace setup plan` to report detected install/dev commands per project, and an explicit execution command such as `devspace setup run <project>` or `devspace setup apply` with confirmation/flags. Support known package managers already detected by scan where practical.
cards.scope: Do not auto-run dependency installs during scan, pull, apply, hydrate, daemon, or FUSE reads. Do not run commands outside validated project paths. Do not run unknown shell snippets without explicit user confirmation. Do not install globally unless explicitly configured and confirmed.

cards.title: DevDrop: final capstone evidence and reflection
cards.task: Finalize the capstone docs after the product stretch and frontier cards land. Update proof artifacts with final command output placeholders, add `docs/capstone/reflection.md`, update the case study with the remote-agent delivery system and frontier work, regenerate `docs/capstone/index.html`, and make the demo script reference the executable demo-check harness. The final docs should clearly distinguish product capabilities from delivery-process evidence.
cards.scope: Documentation and generated HTML only. Do not add product features in this card. Do not fabricate PR, release, or recording URLs; leave explicit placeholders when evidence is not available yet.
dependsOn: DevDrop: release packaging and install docs
DevDrop: hosted manifest sync control plane
DevDrop: background daemon and workspace watcher
DevDrop: FUSE lazy workspace mount prototype
DevDrop: team secret sharing and access model
